findings.title: Collateral cap is unenforceable for pre-upgrade balances in upgraded collateral-cap markets

best_agent_match.title: Collateral cap is bypassed for legacy balances after upgrading to the collateral-cap implementation

findings.title: Flash-loan callers can spoof the `initiator` value delivered to receivers

best_agent_match.title: Flashloan callers can spoof the `initiator` value seen by receivers

rejected_candidates.title: Incorrect Liquidation Health Check Logic
rejected_candidates.reason: The extra `shortfall > 0` check runs after `doTransferIn` but before borrow storage is reduced, so it is not validating post-liquidation health. It is a mid-transfer reentrancy defense, not inverted liquidation logic.

rejected_candidates.title: Flash Loan State Inconsistency Allows Reentrancy
rejected_candidates.reason: During the callback, `totalBorrows` is temporarily increased but `internalCash` has already been reduced by the same loan amount, preserving core exchange-rate accounting. No concrete exploit path or accounting break was substantiated beyond generic reentrancy speculation.

rejected_candidates.title: Unprotected gulp() Function Allows Reserve Manipulation
rejected_candidates.reason: `gulp()` only converts excess underlying already sitting in the contract into reserves. Anyone can already donate tokens to the market, and calling `gulp()` does not create attacker profit, fund loss, or meaningful protocol harm.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Access Control on Collateral Cap Setting
rejected_candidates.reason: `_setCollateralCap` explicitly requires `msg.sender == admin`. This is a style inconsistency at most, not an access-control vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Unused isNative Parameter in doTransferIn/doTransferOut
rejected_candidates.reason: Unused parameters are code smell only and do not create a realistic security impact here.

rejected_candidates.title: Liquidation liveness depends on an extra post-transfer liquidity check inside a callback-capable repayment path
rejected_candidates.reason: This scenario is highly speculative and mainly relies on callback behavior during `transferFrom` to change the borrower's cross-market liquidity mid-transaction. The most direct callback control is with the liquidator/payer, who can already sabotage their own liquidation, so a realistic third-party protocol-level exploit was not established.

rejected_candidates.title: Flashloan callback magic value is non-standard and rejects standard ERC-3156 receivers
rejected_candidates.reason: The mismatch is a compatibility/integration issue, but by itself it does not show realistic fund loss, theft, insolvency, or durable denial of service for the protocol. It is not sufficiently reportable as a security finding on this record.
